headline: Miami seeks redemption against top-ranked Washington
subhead: The Hurricanes visit an undefeated Huskies squad looking to shake off an offensive collapse at LSU.

xFactor.why: Miami's quarterback must limit the self-inflicted errors that cost them 11 points in the LSU loss if they hope to stay competitive in Seattle.

keyMatchup: Miami's secondary against Washington's passing game will determine whether the Huskies can build on their methodical Week 1 victory or if Miami's back-end can create enough pressure to disrupt their rhythm.
body: Miami's 27-38 loss to LSU exposed more than just a 11-point deficit. The Hurricanes committed the kinds of turnovers and costly penalties that flatten road trips against elite opponents, and they're walking into Husky Stadium immediately after. Washington, meanwhile, ground out a 25-18 win over Oklahoma with the kind of controlled execution that suggests Mario Cristobal's program has stabilized the defense-first culture. The Huskies allowed Oklahoma to stay close, but they didn't blink.

For Miami to pull the upset, they'll need their quarterback to play mistake-free football and their defense to generate early pressure that forces Washington into uncomfortable situations. The Huskies' offense doesn't explode for 40 points, but it doesn't need to when their defense is this stingy. Unless Miami can impose their tempo and keep drives alive, this looks like a second straight loss that sends a louder message than the score of Week 1 alone.
